title: oxytropis coerulea subsp. coerulea
intro: Oxytropis coerulea subsp. coerulea, also known as the blue lupine or sky lupine, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, due to its high concentration of isoflavones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Oxytropis coerulea subsp. coerulea include its ability to alleviate symptoms of menopause, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, capsules, or tinctures, and are often used to support women's health and overall well-being.
